title: Course Overview FINS3640
body:
Course Overview
 Basic information
 • Lecturer in Charge
   • Mathieu Fournier or (Math): m.Fournier@unsw.edu.au

 • Teaching Assistant
   • Mengshi Jia: m.jia@student.unsw.edu.au



 • My Background
   • Originally French but lived 18 years in Canada and 2 years in Australia.
   • Ph.D. from University of Toronto
   • Specialization: Development of pricing models, design of investment strategies, asset
    allocation and portfolio construction, etc..
   • Extensive Industry Experiences: Before moving to Australia, I was doing consulting
    as Director for KPMG. With them, I worked with traders and portfolio managers at
    leading banks and pension funds to help them improve the models used for trading,
    investments, portfolio construction, hedging, etc…

                                                                                             2
 Basic information

 • There are no tutorials for this course

 • Weekly Office Hours:
   • Every Friday from 3.00pm to 3.45pm via zoom (link in moodle)
   • First 3 weeks, Mengshi will hold them to help you running and setting-up
    python.

 • What I expect from you:
   • Before the class: Read book chapter(s) before the lecture if any and the slides.
   • During the class: Ask me questions if you do not understand.
   • After the class:
     • Do the exercises provided for each lecture,
     • Read my notes that I will share with you after each class,
     • Go over the Python applications I will share with you.
                                                                                        3
 About the course

 • Objective
   • Deepen your understanding of investments technics and models for most common
    financial securities.
   • Understand the reality of investment and portfolio allocation.
   • Introduce you to Python to perform basic financial data manipulations and modelling.
 • Topics covered
   • Stock and bond pricing, factor models, and investment strategies (weeks 1-5)
   • Bond hedging and trading (weeks 3)
   • Portfolio construction (weeks 7-8)
   • Hedge funds and mutual funds (weeks 9-10)
 • Why This Course is Important?
   • This course is essential for anyone interested in understanding the core investment techniques and
     models that are widely used across the financial industry. The strategies and tools covered are
     foundational to the work of numerous professionals, including those in mutual/superannuation funds,
     hedge funds, trading desks, insurance companies, and beyond. Mastering these techniques enables
     you to make informed decisions in asset allocation, risk management, and portfolio construction,
     equipping you with the skills required to excel in various investment-related roles.

                                                                                                           4
 Learning Materials


 • There is no textbook requirement for this course.

 • The PowerPoint presentations and practice questions will be posted on moodle
   and those should be the core elements of your study materials.

 • I will, when necessary, give you references to read. Most of them will be taken
   from:
   • Bodie, Z., Kane, A., Marcus, A., 2018, Investments, 11th edition, McGraw-Hill Irwin.
     [BKM]
   • A pdf version is available in Moodle.




                                                                                            5
 Assessments

 •Group Assignment 1 (15%)

 •Midterm quiz (35%)

 •Group assignment 2 (15%)

 •Final exam (35%)




                             6
 Assessment

 •Group Assignment 1 (15%)
  • Python
  • Topic:
    • Bond index replication (from Week 3 – Lecture 3)

  • 1-4 students
  • Already released
  • Due in Week 5 (Sunday 11:59pm, 19th October)
  • More information will be given in class in week 3




                                                         7
 Assessment

 •Midterm quiz (35%)
  • In-person via Inspira
  • 1h30 min
  • Week 5: Saturday 18th of October from 9.00am to 10.30am in Rex Vowels
   Theatre (K-F17-LG3)
  • Covers materials from weeks 1-5
  • Similar to recommended exercise questions provided
  • More information will be given in Weeks 4-5.




                                                                            8
 Assessment

 •Group assignment 2 (15%)
  • Python
  • Topic:
    • Portfolio construction (from Week 7 – Lecture 6)
    • Portfolio performance analysis (from Week 8 – lecture 7)

  • 1-4 students, not necessary the same group as assignment 1
  • Released in Week 8
  • Due in Week 10 (Thursday 11:59pm, 20th November)
  • More information will be given after midterm




                                                                 9
 Assessment

 •Final (35%)
  • Time: School exam time.
  • In-person.
  • 1h30min (like midterm).
  • Similar to exercise questions provided.
  • Cover lectures 6-9 (weeks 7-10)
  • More information will be given after the Flex week.




                                                          10
 Moodle

•Course announcements
  • Assumed to be common knowledge

•4 Additional Forums:
  • Lecture content: to ask questions related to the lectures and their content.
  • Exercise: to ask questions and discuss the recommended exercises.
  • Assignment: to discuss and ask questions about the 2 assignments.
  • Forum to find assignment teammates: to find teammates for the 2
   assignments. I will not check this forum which is for your own usage!
    • When using it, provides some details to your classmates about whether you want to
     meet in person or prefer to work remotely, when you plan on working on it (day
     v.s. night), your contact, if you prefer to divide the work or work together, etc..
                                                                                           11
 Moodle

• Forums:
  • The forums are divided so that it makes it easier for you to find relevant
   information.
  • If you have an answer to one of the other student question, share it and help your
   colleagues!
    • Make friends, maybe in the future, they will help you get the job you want: Good things
      happen to good people.
    • When it comes to final grade, and if you are on the margin, your participation in the
      forums may push you to the next grade level. Something to keep in mind.
  • I will oversee them and try to provide answers in a timely manner (within 48
   hours).
  • Since I have online office hours, if the question is not easy to answer using a few
   words/sentences, come ask me the questions you have during my office hours
   instead of using the forums.

                                                                                                12
 Moodle



•Lecture notes
  • Released on Friday around 12pm the week prior to the lecture.



•My personal email is shared with you for urgent/personal matters
  • Try my best to reply in 48 hours
  • If it is not urgent nor personal, use the forums or ask me at the end of the
   class or during my online office hours.




                                                                                   13
